entry.title: Re: Any lessons learned from connecting SF EC to ADP Workforce Now using SF Integration Center?
entry.content:
In my opinion and experience, building it with Integration Centre was very difficult. Rather we couldn't achieve delta using that. So we built a custom interface on SAP Integration Suite for integration between SuccessFactors Employee Central and ADP Workforce.

For our custom iflow we took inspiration from the below existing community template for third party payroll integration. 

https://github.com/SAP/apibusinesshub-integration-recipes/blob/master/Recipes/for/sapsuccessfactorsemployeecentralwiththirdpartypayrollvendor/ConfigGuide-SFECto3rdPartyPayroll-TemplateA.pdf

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hi David,

 

I would like to learn more about SF as I am working as a project manager for this part. I am currently scheduled for the exam (SAP Certified Specialist - Project Manager - SAP Activate for Agile Implementation Management).

I am keen to expand my knowledge more to be come more competent in SAP.

 

Thank you

entry.title: How to control at goods receipt time only PO scheduled line quantity.
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

How can I control the PO schedule line quantity based on the scheduled dates defined on the 'Delivery Schedule' tab? For example, I have defined four different delivery dates and want to control these dates at the goods receipt level. If the supplier sends more than 50 quantities on 26.09.2024, it should trigger an error for each date. Goods receipt should only be done on their respective due dates.

Thanks

Ahmed

entry.title: Re: Using SAP Companion Extended Scenario in S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hi Kavinprabu,

There are many parameters that can be added to the SAP Companion configuration to modify the behaviour of SAP Companion.  The details of these parameters are located in the SAP Companion Integration Guide.  In particular, look at the section that relates to the product that you wish to use SAP Companion in, e.g. S/4HANA, and the Features and Configuration section.  The other sections are also important but these two sections are VERY important.

At a minimum level, the parameters that are required to use SAP Companion to distribute standard and customer-specific content are located in the instructions for your specific application.  For example, if I were turning on SAP Companion for a recent version of SAP S/4HANA On Premise (also called Private Cloud) I would look at these instructions:

 When you access the relevant section of instructions, consider the parameters defined in the Parameters Independent of Content Scenario and Parameters for Extended Content sections.  You need to add all the parameters from both sections.

Finally, to get Enable Now content to appear in SAP Companion, you must add the following two parameters in addition:

learningAppWorkspace=WorkareaID (change WorkareaID to the ID of the Workarea in Manager that contains your customer-specific cotnent)
learningAppBackendUrl=https://CustomerName.enable-now.cloud.sap/ (change CustomerName to the domain name of your SAP Enable Now system)

If this is still failing, add the editor=true parameter to the configuration to turn on the editing function.  This should help to show content if it is available.

Outside that, paste a screenshot of your configuration in this post, along with the system that you can using, e.g. S/4HANA, and I will try to identify the issue.

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Re: Error when exporting to executable file in SEN
entry.content:
Hi Mani,

This is typically caused because you have already generated a version of the exe file into the same folder that you are now trying to generate a new version into.  Make sure that the Windows folder that you are generating the exe file into is empty.

Regards
Shane

There is a standard app Stock Overview - Mainland China, Taiwan

entry.title: Re: Balance carryforward of actual data from SAP to SAC P...

Dear

have you referenced one of the documents from SAP Rapid Financial Planning & Analysis for SAP S/4HANA (2EB)

There is flow type: BSENDING Ending Balance of Balance Sheet Accounts

can be used to extract ending balance

Regards,

Sen

entry.title: Number Range objects
entry.content:
Hi,

I am using number range object in one of my application developed using sap rap in BTP environment.

As soon as I created the number range object, it shows up in ' Manage Number Range Interval' app for me to setup the intervals. But after transporting it to the customer system, I do not see this object in their system.

I checked to make sure I have similar roles in our system and in customer system. Not sure what I am missing.

Appreciate any inputs

entry.title: Re: An Implementation of Text-to-SQL with AI
entry.content: Thank you, very good blog and really inspiring.

entry.title: Problemas na troca da base de dados
entry.content:
Possuo um relatorio que foi desenvolvido em uma base de dados chamada SMS, que atendia a um cliente especifico. Os outros clientes rodam relatorios na base de dados chamada SGM. No meio do ano um dos clientes da base SGM comprou a empresa que utilizava a Base de dados  SMS. As bases são identicas, mas foi solicitado pelo cliente comprador que unificaasse a base de dados para a SGM. 
As bases são iguais, com a mesma estrutura matendo apenas o nomes diferentes. 

Alguns relatorios foram desenvolvidos especilamente para base SMS, que ficava em outro servidor.

Ao trazer os arquivos do crystal ele abre normalmente, permite conectar a nova base de dados da: SMS para SGM, mas quando roda, não traz nenhuma infomaçao.

Ao verificar a consulta SQL no menu "banco de dados", notei que mesmo escolhendo a base SGM a consulta continua trazendo a base de dados SMS e não atuasliza pela SGM de nenhuma maneira. 
Como a consulta SQL não pode ser editada, existe alguma maneira de resolver esse problema ?

entry.title: Re: Migration of EHANA to Datasphere by leveraging Native HANA Cloud Instance of Datasphere Tenant
entry.content:
@Vivek-RR  Thank you for your input. To gain a clearer understanding of the risks associated with this migration approach, could you please elaborate on the specific concerns SAP foresees when migrating HANA calculation views to the HANA Cloud instance of Datasphere?

Given that if scenario involves only a limited number of calculation views and doesn't represent an advanced analytics use case (such as APL, PAL, or the Graph Engine), it would be helpful to understand why SAP considers this an unsuitable approach. Are there particular technical challenges, performance bottlenecks, or limitations in terms of compatibility and functionality that we should be aware of?

Your insights will be invaluable for everyone following this blog who is eager to learn more about this topic. This will also ensure that people align their migration strategy with SAP’s recommended practices. Thank you in advance for your guidance. 

@shabana please feel free to share your perspective, as you are a member of the product team and may have additional insights into the future direction and any associated risks related to this topic.

entry.title: How to access SAP Cloud ALM subaccount in BTP cockpit.
entry.content:
Please share the URL of SAP Cloud ALM subaccount in BTP cockpit.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to access SAP Cloud ALM subaccount in BTP cockpit.
entry.content:
Hello Customer,

To access BTP Cockpit you can use the following URL:

Access:  https://cockpit.btp.cloud.sap

Depending on your own GEO location this URL will redirect you to the closest of the three regional Cockpit URLs
(https://emea.cockpit.btp.cloud.sap, https://amer.cockpit.btp.cloud.sap, https://apac.cockpit.btp.cloud.sap).

Then you can select the Global Account of Cloud ALM.
  • Global account named "SAP Cloud ALM" or "SAP Cloud ALM - <ERP Cust Num>".
  • Sub account named "SAP Cloud ALM".
    • This Sub account will be under the "SAP Cloud ALM" Global account.

Best regards,
Ellie Jia
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Migration of EHANA to Datasphere by leveraging Native HANA Cloud Instance of Datasphere Tenant
entry.content:
@Vivek-RR  Thank you for your input. To gain a clearer understanding of the risks associated with this migration approach, could you please elaborate on the specific concerns SAP foresees when migrating HANA calculation views to the HANA Cloud instance of Datasphere?

Given that if scenario involves only a limited number of calculation views and doesn't represent an advanced analytics use case (such as APL, PAL, or the Graph Engine), it would be helpful to understand why SAP considers this an unsuitable approach. Are there particular technical challenges, performance bottlenecks, or limitations in terms of compatibility and functionality that we should be aware of?

Your insights will be invaluable for everyone following this blog who is eager to learn more about this topic. This will also ensure that people align their migration strategy with SAP’s recommended practices. Thank you in advance for your guidance. 

@shabana please feel free to share your perspective, as you are a member of the product team and may have additional insights into the future direction and any associated risks related to this topic.

entry.title: Re: Migration of EHANA to Datasphere by leveraging Native HANA Cloud Instance of Datasphere Tenant
entry.content:
@Vivek-RR  Thank you for your input. To gain a clearer understanding of the risks associated with this migration approach, could you please elaborate on the specific concerns SAP foresees when migrating HANA calculation views to the HANA Cloud instance of Datasphere?

Given that if scenario involves only a limited number of calculation views and doesn't represent an advanced analytics use case (such as APL, PAL, or the Graph Engine), it would be helpful to understand why SAP considers this an unsuitable approach. Are there particular technical challenges, performance bottlenecks, or limitations in terms of compatibility and functionality that we should be aware of?

Your insights will be invaluable for everyone following this blog who is eager to learn more about this topic. This will also ensure that people align their migration strategy with SAP’s recommended practices. Thank you in advance for your guidance. 

@shabana please feel free to share your perspective, as you are a member of the product team and may have additional insights into the future direction and any associated risks related to this topic.

entry.title: Re: Migration of EHANA to Datasphere by leveraging Native HANA Cloud Instance of Datasphere Tenant
entry.content:
@Vivek-RR , thank you for your input. To better understand the risks associated with this migration approach, could you please elaborate on the specific concerns SAP foresees when migrating HANA calculation views to the HANA Cloud instance of Datasphere?

Considering that if this scenario involves only a limited number of calculation views and doesn’t represent an advanced analytics use case (like APL, PAL, or the Graph Engine), it would be helpful to know why SAP views this approach as unsuitable. Are there specific technical challenges, performance issues, or compatibility limitations that we should be mindful of?

Your insights will be extremely valuable to everyone following this discussion and eager to learn more. 

@shabana , as a member of the product team, please feel free to add your perspective, as you may have additional insights into the future direction and any potential risks tied to this topic.

Balakrishna

entry.title: Re: Financial Statements F.01 for S/4 HANA
entry.content:
Dear All,

I got the way and sharing here for the person who has the same question like me and myself as note.

Step1:

 

Step2:

Step3:

Open the Excel as new document and open the file which is download at Step2 as below excel menu.

 

Step4:

Data->Data get from "Text or CSV"

"Open" the text file which you download by Step 2 and "Import" them " Read".

Step5:

You can get the statement as query data, so change the normal data if need it.

Table design -> Change the standard Cell 

I hope this help to someone who wanted to know this way.

Best regards,

entry.title: How to create a matrix report in story reporting

I need to create a report with depicts in matrix format position title for job level and department. 

thank you,

Naveen

entry.title: Re: SAP S4 HANA Business Partner (BP) end to end configurations
entry.content:
Thank you @Senthilkumar3 for your clear and comprehensive explanation. Having recently upgraded from ECC to S4, this was extremely helpful in understanding what to configure when creating a new Customer Account Group, to ensure we do the right thing with respect to the novel BP object!

A tiny correction... in Section 8 Master Data Synchronisation
Remove "Synchronization Control" from
1) the IMG menu path (which should read Master Data Synchronization --> Customer/Vendor Integration) and
2) remove the highlight from"Synchronization Control" in the screenshot

More importantly, I would HIGHLIGHT in Section 2 "Create Number Ranges for Vendor Accounts" that, if vendors are expected to be automatically created with the SAME vendor number as the Business Partner number created via transaction BP, then the Vendor Account Group number range must be EXTERNAL. Your screenshot shows the "EXT" flag set, and you DO say, in Section 6, that "If you want to create BP code supplier as the same, then give the same number range which is given to the account group number range", but the very important prerequisite of the Vendor Account Group number range being EXTERNAL is not apparent to your readers - who might otherwise only become aware of the prerequisite if they happened to click on the (very obscure) F1-Help for the "Same Numbers" checkbox in the BP Group-to-Account Group assignment (V_TBD001-XSAMENUMBER) - which, in the last step of Section 8, you say to "enable", but again, don't emphasise the prerequisite stated in the F1-Help "External number assignment is set in Financial Accounting (FI)"

Once again, thank you for your time and effort in posting a very helpful explanation. Your generosity will come back to you in many different ways!

entry.title: Can anyone explain me 'profitability analysis' wrt SAP PS?
Why is my custom BTE 1005 does not hold the value?

Why does that happen?

entry.title: Re: This WBS element is already assigned to another sales...
entry.content:
Hi Seb,

Kindly try the 2 options mentioned below,

1. Uncheck Multiple Sales Order Items Scenario and try to to assign the WBS/Project.
2. Kindly check Revenue Recognition Key must be in sync with WBS and Sales Order.

Regards,

Sharan Padshetty

entry.title: Re: RAP CDS annotation based custom action triggering aut...
entry.content:
Hi,

Navigation is automatically triggered if the action is triggered against a single instance and the action returns a different instance (of the same entity) - see section "Navigation After Executing an Action" in page. This navigation can be disabled though using a manifest setting in Fiori elements OData V4 - see the V4 specific section in the same page that shows the usage of "navigateToInstance" manifest property.

Best Regards,

   Sandeep

I guess what you need is the preferredMode=create URL parameter as described in page. Do also see additional options with this in the V4 specific section in case you are using SAP Fiori elements for OData V4.

   Sandeep

entry.title: Re: ABAP Calling last calling program in the stack
entry.content: For some reason you don't want to give us the whole code of module user_command_400. Maybe you can add an info message direct after the call screen (message 'Some text.' type 'I'.). Do you see this message after returning from the called transaction?

entry.title: Re: Use of .mdf and .ldf file, SAP Business One SQL
entry.content:
Hi nikunjmehta2290,

MDF file is the primary data file of a SQL database.

The . LDF file is the transaction log file of a SQL database. The transaction log is used to guarantee the data integrity of the database and for data recovery.

Kind regards,

ANKIT CHAUHAN

SAP Business One Support

entry.title: Attachment size control
entry.content: Customer wants to limit user to only upload file size eg. max. 5mb and does not want user to upload large image file. Can we restrict the attachment file upload size in S4Hana Cloud Public Edition? If yes, can it be controlled by file formats? Any limitation on the attachment upload formats?

Regards,

Venkata Suri.

Once you have the patch package, you can install DTW from \Packages.x64\Data Transfer Workbench.

Kind regards,

ANKIT CHAUHAN

entry.title: SAP Ariba: Contracts why is there no Learning System

Thank you

entry.title: SAP Ariba Contracts Certification Contains lessons that say Sourcing
entry.content:
Hi,

I am studing the SAP Ariba Contracts implementation consultant certification. As part of the lessons that need to be studied it has two modules that refer to SAP Ariba Strategic Sourcing. I have been through the lessons and it seems that the content is generic across the strategic sourcing suite which I think includes Contracts.

Can anyone confirm that this isn't an error and I do have the correct modules showing. It is quite confusing when you see the titles especially as SAP Ariba Sourcing is a certification in itself.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Challenges with Field Extraction in Indian Invoices U...
entry.content:
You need to create individual document templates for each different format and don't use created schema , Try to use OCR with AI capability,

Other way you will try to get data by using inbuild OCR(PDF) for failed documents in Pretrained model and compare results.

I think you can get better results

entry.title: Re: Issue for getting Output Format for a SQVI query
entry.content:
hi

you have restricted output format to one option.

go to SQVI, change Export as, save

and you will have more output formats.

entry.title: Document change records
entry.content: Is there any automation archive function for the change logs in system? For example, there are lots of Output record created when sales order is changed. Our customer is concerning will the system out of storage space due to these log files.

entry.title: Re: How to allow an empty string with check condition in xml transformation?
entry.content:
Ok, i would try to describe.
There are 4 options of how <branchCode> can attend in document
1) No <branchCode> in document
2) empty branchCode <branchCode></branchCode>
3) something inside branchCode <branchCode>value</branchCode>
4) space inside branchCode <branchCode> </branchCode>
When i to "Deserialize " xml with space inside, i'm getting a dump, when it's space inside <branchCode> (fourth option)
if i delete the 's-check' i will have a dump in case, where document hasn't <branchCode> inside (first option).
so how can i save mine s-check condition and allow to fill BRANCHCODE  with space?

entry.title: Re: Understanding on Resource Capacity SAP Business One
entry.content:
Hi @nikunjmehta2290 ,

What is "Time per Resource Unit"?
"Time per Resource Unit" refers to the amount of time required to process one unit of a product or service using a specific resource (such as machinery, labor, or equipment). It is a measure of efficiency and helps in planning and scheduling production activities.

Where is it Used in SAP B1 HANA?
Production Orders: In production orders, this metric is used to estimate the time required for completing a production run. This helps in scheduling and ensures that resources are allocated efficiently.

Resource Planning: It is used in the resource planning module to determine the capacity requirements of various resources (like machines or work centers) based on the time needed per unit. This helps in optimizing the use of resources and avoiding bottlenecks.

Cost Calculation: In cost accounting and financial planning, the time per resource unit helps in calculating labor and overhead costs associated with production. This is crucial for pricing strategies and profitability analysis.

Manufacturing and Production Scheduling: It aids in creating realistic production schedules by taking into account the time required for each operation. This ensures that deadlines are met and production goals are achieved.

Capacity Planning: It helps in assessing whether the available resources can handle the production load, allowing businesses to adjust their capacity or make informed decisions about investments in additional resources.

What is "Res. Units per Time Period"?
"Resource Units per Time Period" refers to the number of units that a resource (such as machinery, equipment, or labor) can process or handle within a specified time period. This is essentially a measure of the capacity or throughput of a resource. For instance, if a machine can produce 100 units per hour, then the "Res. Units per Time Period" for that machine might be set as 100 units per hour.

Where is it Used in SAP B1 HANA?
Resource Planning and Scheduling:

Capacity Planning: Helps in determining how many units of a product can be produced by a resource within a given time period. This is crucial for aligning production schedules with resource availability.
Work Center Setup: In work centers, this metric helps in calculating the capacity of the work center and scheduling tasks accordingly.
Production Orders:

Estimation and Scheduling: When creating and managing production orders, the system uses "Res. Units per Time Period" to estimate how long a production run will take and how many units can be produced in that time frame. This helps in planning and meeting deadlines.
Cost Analysis and Budgeting:

Cost Allocation: Understanding the throughput of resources helps in allocating costs more accurately. For example, if a resource’s throughput affects the cost structure, knowing how many units it can process per period helps in budgeting and cost management.
Capacity Utilization:

Optimization: By knowing the resource capacity in terms of units per time period, businesses can optimize resource usage, identify bottlenecks, and make informed decisions about resource allocation and adjustments.
How to Use It?
In SAP B1 HANA, you typically configure "Res. Units per Time Period" in the resource master data. Here’s how it might be utilized:

Set Up Resource Data: Define the resource and specify its throughput capacity in units per time period in the resource or work center master data.
Plan Production: Use this data to plan production runs, taking into account the capacity of each resource.
Schedule and Monitor: Apply this information to schedule production orders and monitor whether resources are operating within their capacity limits.

How Daily Capacity Factors Work
Definition: For each resource or work center, you can define the capacity available each day of the week. For example, a machine might be available for 8 hours on weekdays but only 4 hours on Saturdays.

Configuration: In SAP B1 HANA, you configure Daily Capacity Factors in the master data for work centers or resources. This involves specifying the number of available working hours or units per time period for each day of the week.

Capacity Calculation:

Resource Utilization: The system uses these factors to calculate how much work a resource can handle each day. If a resource is less available on certain days, the capacity calculations are adjusted accordingly.
Production Scheduling: When scheduling production orders, SAP B1 uses the Daily Capacity Factors to distribute the workload over the available days, ensuring that the production plan aligns with the actual availability of resources.
Capacity Planning:

Realistic Planning: By accounting for different daily capacities, businesses can create more accurate production plans and schedules. This helps in avoiding overloading resources on days when their capacity is lower.
Optimization: It helps in optimizing the use of resources, reducing downtime, and improving overall production efficiency.

How to Configure Daily Capacity Factors
Access the Work Center Master Data: Go to the master data for work centers or resources.
Define Capacity: For each work center, specify the Daily Capacity Factors for each day of the week. This includes setting the available hours or units per day.
Apply and Save: Save the configuration. The system will now use these factors in production planning and scheduling.

entry.title: Re: Moving from Solman to C-ALM, what about users with no external network
entry.content: Hello Greg,

entry.title: Change log Control
entry.content:
Why S4hana cloud public edition has IMG setting "Fields relevant to printouts of changes" on change log control for purchase order, but no configure on sales order? 

 

Any standard setting can control the fields for change logs in sales documents?

entry.title: Re: MRP for Jobworker in SAP B1
entry.content:
Hi @TanviV ,

Steps to Manage MRP for Job Work in SAP B1 HANA

  1. Define the Bill of Materials (BOM) and Routing:
    • BOM: Ensure you have a Bill of Materials defined for the Finished Goods (FG) that you receive from the Job Worker. This BOM should include both the raw materials and any semi-finished goods (if applicable) that you send to the Job Worker.
    • Routing: Define any routing if your production process involves multiple steps or operations.
  2. Set Up the Job Work (Subcontracting) in SAP B1:
    • Create a Subcontracting Item: In the Item Master Data, create an item for the Finished Goods with the appropriate attributes and ensure it’s set up to be processed as a subcontracting item.
    • Define the Job Work Process: Go to Production > Production Order and create a production order for the Finished Goods. In the order, specify the Job Worker as the supplier for the subcontracting process.
  3. Configure MRP Settings:
    • Material Requirements Planning (MRP): Go to MRP > Run MRP to start the planning process. Ensure that your settings are configured to handle subcontracting scenarios. This might involve configuring MRP parameters to consider the raw materials needed for subcontracting and the requirements for in-house production.
  4. Procure Raw Materials:
    • Purchase Orders: Once MRP has processed, it should recommend purchase orders for the raw materials needed. Ensure you’ve set the procurement type for these raw materials to “Purchase” in the Item Master Data.
  5. Process the Production Orders:
    • Semi-Finished Goods: If your process involves making semi-finished goods in-house, create production orders for these items as well. This should be reflected in your BOM and routing.
    • Send to Job Worker: Once the semi-finished goods are produced, you need to send them to the Job Worker. Create a Delivery for the semi-finished goods and link it to the Job Work (subcontracting) process.
  6. Track Job Work (Subcontracting) Orders:
    • Goods Receipt from Job Worker: Once the Job Worker has completed the Finished Goods, you will receive them back. Perform a Goods Receipt to update your inventory with the Finished Goods from the Job Worker.
  7. Review MRP Recommendations:
    • MRP Reports: Check the MRP recommendations to ensure that all necessary raw materials and semi-finished goods are planned and ordered appropriately.

entry.title: Re: Special GL

entry.title: Customize printout format

We checked on the Adobe LiveCycle Designer, but seems can not find the change values relevant field can be used? Any standard approach/ workarounds?

Dear Savio_D,

entry.title: Filter empty xml payload and send reply back to sender
entry.content:
Hi All

We have an iflow ,C4C -> ODATA->BPA . We are receiving json payload from C4C then we enrich the payload using ODATA .The ODATA has multiple line items hence we are using splitter to split the payload inside local interation process.

The current requirement is to initimate the sender(C4C) if any xml field is empty saying "The necessary fields are missing" and end the iflow ,else send the payload to BPA.

Initially thought of filtering after the splitter not sure what kind of xpath condition to add.

Please pour your suggestions on this .

entry.title: Re: Performance Monitoring in SAP Datasphere

Thanks.

Regards

entry.title: Re: Embedding S4 Custom Fiori app inside BTP Fiori app
entry.content:
Hi Siddharth,

is your BTP Hosted Freestyle App provided via the SAP Build Work Zone Launchpad? If yes I would suggest that you add the S/4HANA on Prem app by following the documentation Content Providers - On-Premise Solutions and link to it using semantic navigation. Embedding it into the Freestyle App would be possible, but I think you should not do so as it isn't a good user experience.

Best Regards
Gregor

entry.title: Re: Split valuation is not getting updated in EWM table /SCWM/T_VAL_SPLT

Any reason for this or I am missing something?

Thanks,

Onkar

entry.title: Re: SAP extends ERP cloud portfolio with "SAP ERP, private edition, option for Microsoft SQL&qu
entry.content:
@chinadad96- You are correct - except one thing. This is NOT a RISE offering, it is a specific private cloud offering. It does include an ECC 6.0 system landscape only, no additional components as in RISE with SAP including as main component SAP S/4HANA.

Summarized:

  • ECC 6.0 EhP 0-5 will go out of mainstream maintenance by end of 2025 with no option to move to extended maintenance. It will then move into customer-specific maintenance.
  • ECC 6.0 EhP 6-8 will go out of mainstream maintenance by end of 2027 with the option to move to extended maintenance until end of 2030. Extended maintenance is included in the baseline subscription fee.
  • Hyperscaler is Microsoft Azure.
  • Main components (ECC+MSSQL) on Windows only.

entry.title: Reverse Payments and Postings from TRM Financial Transactions (1) - via Reversal of Transaction
entry.content:
Foreword

This article Introduces how to reverse payments and postings for cash flows from financial transactions created in Treasury and Risk Management (TRM) solution of S/4HANA Public Cloud, via reversal of transaction.

Notes:

  • This article only introduces the reversal of payments and postings for operative flows (which is relevant to payment) in financial transactions. Reversal of payments and postings of cash flows arising from securities account management (SAM) is not covered by this article.
  • This article only introduces the cases that payment request is generated for payment relevant flows. Reversal of payment for vendor/customer is not covered by this article.
  • To enhance readability, this article utilizes transaction codes (such as TBB1, TPM10, TPM13) wherever possible, instead of mentioning the full application names.

Introduction

To reverse the payment and posting for a fixed flow of OTC transaction, sometimes you have to ‘reverse’ the financial transaction itself and revert it to the previous activity (e.g. from activity category 'Contract Settlement' to 'Contract').

Usually this method must be used for reversal of the following flows (but not limited to them):

Product Category of Transaction

Cash Flow

Processing Cat. of Transaction Type

Activity Category Change thru. Reversal of Transaction

550 - Interest Rate Instrument

Initial principal increase (at the inception of transaction)

00002 - With Settlement

from 'Contract Settlement' to 'Contract'

600 - FX Transaction

Buy / sell foreign exchange

00101 - Order - Contract – Settlement

from 'Contract Settlement' to 'Contract'

 

Cash settlement of Deliverable Forward

00101 - Order - Contract – Settlement

from 'Contract Settlement' to 'Contract'

 

Cash settlement of Non-deliverable Forward

00121 - Order - Contract - Settlement - Fixing - Settlement

from 'Fixing Settlement' to 'Fixing’

760 - OTC Options

Premium

00101 - Order-Contr.-Settlmnt-Exer./Expir.-Settlmnt-Termintn-TSettl.

from 'Contract Settlement' to 'Contract'

 

Cash settlement (for exercised option)

00101 - Order-Contr.-Settlmnt-Exer./Expir.-Settlmnt-Termintn-TSettl.

00102 - Order - Contract - Exercise/Expriration - Settlement

from 'Exercise Settlement' to 'Exercise'

850 - Letter of Credit

Payment Obligation

00001 - Without Settlement

00002 - With Settlement

from 'Settled' to 'Accepted'/’Draft’ for specific presentation*

 

Acceptance Payment

00001 - Without Settlement

00002 - With Settlement

from 'Settled' to 'Accepted'/’Draft’ for specific presentation*

* In a strict sense, reversing the presentation status of Letter of Credit is not reverting to the previous activity of transaction.

Example:

  1. A transaction for Interest Rate Instrument (product category 550) has been created, it is now with activity category ‘Contract Settlement’.
  2. The payment request for the flow of initial principal increase (flow type 1100-) has been generated via TBB1 or TBB1_PAY, but payment request has not been processed by F111 (payment request is ‘open’).
  3. The postings for the position flows of initial principal increase (update type MM1100-) have been carried out via TBB1 or TPM10 for all valuation areas.
  4. Accounting clerk realizes that the G/L account used for recognizing initial principal increase is incorrect, and the house bank/account ID used in payment request is incorrect, therefore the relevant postings and payment request should be reversed.

TM_53:

F8BT: payment request is ‘Open’.

TPM13: status of position flows (in all valuation areas) of the initial principal increase is ‘F’ (Fixed).

TPM20: posting journal entries are available for the initial principal increase in all valuation areas.

TPM20A: payment journal has status ‘Created’.

Target:

Reverse the payment request and postings for this initial principal flow.

Step-by-Step Guide:

1. Access app Manage Financial Transactions, identify the transaction number, and then choose the ‘Reverse’ option to revert this transaction to previous activity.

On the next screen, choose Enter.

On the next screen, select a Reversal Reason, and then choose Save.

2. Check in TM_53: this transaction has been reverted to previous activity category ‘Contract’, and the Fixing Status, Payment Status, Posting Status of cash flow has returned to initial statuses.

TPM13: previously ‘F’ (fixed) position flows have the status ‘ToR’ (To be Reversed) now.

3. Assess TPM10, enter the necessary parameters, and choose Run.

On the next screen, choose Execute.

Check posting and payment reversal logs and messages.

(you can see similar information in TPM20A)

4. Check in F8BT: now the payment request can only be displayed when ‘Cleared’ option is selected.

This payment request is now flagged with Reverse = “X“, and this payment request can no longer be processed by F111.

Check in TPM13: now relevant position flows have status ‘R’ (Reversed)

 Check in TPM20: reversal journal entries have been created.

Click the Object Key, the detailed journal entry will be displayed in another screen.

Original (reversed) journal entry in leading ledger:

Reversal journal entry in leading ledger:

5. After the postings and payment request are reversed, configuration expert fixes the G/L account determination configuration, and front office fixes the house bank/account ID to be used in this transaction. The next step is to settle the transaction again (which can be done in app Manage Financial Transactions), and re-run TBB1_PAY / TBB1 / TPM10 to generate payment request and postings again. As this is not the focus of this article, further elaboration is not provided here.

Caution:

This method (via reversal of transaction) should be applied judiciously.

For instance, once a transaction is reversed and its activity category shifts from 'Contract Settlement' back to 'Contract', usually all associated fixed position flows in this transaction will have the 'ToR' (To Be Reversed) status (can check in TPM13), these position flows will then require reversal via TPM10, which may inadvertently result in the reversal of flows that are unnecessarily to be reversed.

To accurately identify and reverse the specific flow(s) in a transaction (when it is possible), please refer to the article Reverse Payments and Postings from TRM Financial Transactions (2) - via Reversal of Specific Cash Flow in Transaction for detailed guidance.

Siddharth

entry.title: Re: SAP Community - Where are you from?
entry.content: I Am In Nigeria, And Am very Proud Of My Country.

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Apps - build service not working since 24 hours
entry.content:
Receiving similar error with new runtime version: 

AB version: 2.13.4
COMMAND: 

NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=16000  npxrnv export -p android -s standalone -c standalone --ci --yes --skipRnvCheck --packageManager yarn --template @sapappgyver/orchestra-template-standalone@4.11.167 -r 

FAILED with ERROR: 

Command failed with exit code 1: npx rnv export -p android -s standalone -c standalone --ci --yes --skipRnvCheck --packageManager yarn --template @sapappgyver/orchestra-template-standalone@4.11.167 -r

entry.title: Re: How to add oAttachments2 to oPurchaseQuotations using...
entry.content:
Hi SAP_Check,

AttachmentEntry accepts integer value only. Also, it will accept the existing value from OATC table.

It is required to have the entry related to the attachment in the OATC table first. Then only, it can be referred to attach.

Hope it helps!

Kind regards,

ANKIT CHAUHAN

SAP Business One Support

entry.title: Re: Maintenance call dates
entry.content:
Hi Preethi,

Could you check the calculation log for the generated call object?

You will find this particular call in the plan, where you can choose the specific call and see the calculation details. Here you will find the date calculation details.

I am not sure, but also check if there is an impact of the leap year.

Regards,

Vijay

entry.title: Reverse Payments and Postings from TRM Transactions (2) - via Reversal of Specific Cash Flow
entry.content:
Foreword

This article Introduces how to reverse payments and postings for cash flows from financial transactions created in Treasury and Risk Management (TRM) solution of S/4HANA Public Cloud, via Reversal of Specific Cash Flow in Transaction.

Notes:

  • This article only introduces the reversal of payments and postings for operative flows (which is relevant to payment) in financial transactions. Reversal of payments and postings of cash flows arising from securities account management (SAM) is not covered by this article.
  • This article only introduces the cases that payment request is generated for payment relevant flows. Reversal of payment for vendor/customer is not covered by this article.
  • To enhance readability, this article utilizes transaction codes (such as TBB1, TPM10, TPM13) wherever possible, instead of mentioning the full application names.

Introduction

Reverse Payments and Postings from TRM Transactions (1) - via Reversal of Transaction introduces that sometimes the transaction has to be reversed (returned to previous activity category) in order to reverse payments and postings arising from a flow in the transaction. This method has its applicable scenarios as well as its limitations.

This article will introduce another method, in which a specific cash flow in a transaction is chosen to be reversed, and then the payment request and postings associated with this specific cash flow can be reversed via TPM10. This method is applicable to reversal of the following flows (but not limited to them):

Product Category of Transaction

Cash Flow

550 - Interest Rate Instrument

Subsequent principal increase/decrease following the initial principal increase

 

Interests

 

Repayments

620 - Swap

Initial principal exchange of cross-currency swap

 

Subsequent principal exchange of cross-currency swap

 

Interest exchange

All

Other Flows (entered in the ‘Other Flows’, like commission)

Example:

  1. A transaction for Interest Rate Instrument (product category 550) has been created, it is now with activity category ‘Contract Settlement’.
  2. The payment request for the flow of nominal interest for an interest period (flow type 1200-) has been generated via TBB1 or TBB1_PAY, but payment request has not been processed by F111 (payment request is ‘open’).
  3. The postings for the position flows of this nominal interest (update type MM1200-) have been carried out via TBB1 or TPM10 for all valuation areas.
  4. Accounting clerk realizes that the G/L account used for recognizing this nominal interest is incorrect, and the house bank/account ID used in payment request is incorrect, therefore the relevant postings and payment request should be reversed.

TM_53:

F8BT: payment request is ‘Open’.

TPM13: status of position flows (in all valuation areas) of this nominal interest is ‘F’ (Fixed).

TPM20: posting journal entries are available for this nominal interest in all valuation areas.

Target:

Reverse the payment request and postings for this nominal interest flow explicitly.

Step-by-Step Guide:

1. Access app Manage Financial Transactions, identify the transaction number, and then choose the ‘Edit’ button.

Go to the Cash Flow tab, and then choose Editing Mode -> Reverse.

Mark the cash flow of nominal interest, and then choose Flows -> Reverse Flow.

Afterwards, a message ‘Flows(s) is/are reversed’ appears, and the posting information of this cash flow is changed to ‘Posting flagged for reversal’ from ‘Posting carried out’. Choose Save.

2. Check in TM_53: have an overview of all cash flows, within which only the original nominal interest flow has flagged as ‘Reversed’, and a new nominal interest flow is now available with posting status ‘Flagged for Fixing’ and payment status ‘Not paid - flagged for payment’.

TPM13: previously ‘F’ (fixed) position flows of the nominal interest have the status ‘ToR’ (To be Reversed) now.

3. Assess TPM10, enter the necessary parameters, and choose Run.

On the next screen, choose Execute.

Check posting and payment reversal logs and messages.

(you can see similar information in TPM20A)

4. Check in TM_53: the reversed nominal interest flow now has posting information ‘Posting reversed’ and payment status ‘Not paid – payment reversed’.

Check in F8BT: now the payment request can only be displayed when ‘Cleared’ option is selected.

This payment request is now flagged with Reverse = “X“, and this payment request can no longer be processed by F111.

Check in TPM13: now the position flows of the nominal interest have status ‘R’ (Reversed)

Check in TPM20: reversal journal entries have been created.

Click the Object Key, the detailed journal entry will be displayed in another screen.

Original (reversed) journal entry in leading ledger:

Reversal journal entry in leading ledger:

5. After the postings and payment request are reversed for this nominal interest flow, configuration expert fixes the G/L account determination configuration for nominal interest, and front office fixes the house bank/account ID to be used in this transaction for interest payment. The next step is to re-run TBB1_PAY / TBB1 / TPM10 to generate payment request and postings for new nominal interest flow. As this is not the focus of this article, further elaboration is not provided here.

entry.title: Create a task for the content publishing in Scheduler
entry.content:
Hi, could you help me please, I'm trying to create a task for the content publishing in Win Scheduler.

And I have got such error

I think something wrong with argument syntax in task 

I use this one:

"C:\Program Files (x86)\SAP Enable Now\Producer 10\producer.exe"

-wa "C:\Users\dn\Producer Workareas\HGSAP" -deploy:BC8F5BB40E301290

entry.title: Set AS2 Filename in receiver channel - BTP Integration Suite
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We need to post the files to partner using AS2 adapter along with the file extension. The channel configurations are supporting only filename & timestamp in B2B TPM. Did anyone came across this scenario, please help me with the solution.

For example: The file name should be EDI_20240913.txt

Thanks in Advance.

entry.title: Re: Using Job Scheduler in SAP Cloud Platform [1]: First simple scenario
entry.content:
The console log, or other logging you are doing inside your application can be fetched using the standard CF CLI: 

cf logs APP_NAME --recent


Or by viewing them in the BTP Cockpit UI - Go to "Cloud Foundry" -> "Spaces" -> click on your space, then applications page will open -> choose an application -> go to "Logs"

In this example - simple synchronous call what is send to the Job Scheduling Service is:

res.send('Finished job');

You can check the other tutorials to see how you can call Job Scheduling service API from your app and can update the execution logs.
